title: Chrysanthemum pseudanthemis
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum pseudanthemis, also known as the pyrethrum or dalmatian chrysanthemum, is classified within the plant family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Chrysanthemum, but some classifications group it under the genus Tanacetum. The exact classification can vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a member of the subfamily Asteroideae. Within the Asteraceae family, it is closely related to other species such as the common daisy and the sunflower. The classification of Chrysanthemum pseudanthemis is still subject to some debate among botanists.
uses: Chrysanthemum pseudanthemis is a plant species that has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Asian cultures. Its flowers and leaves are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In traditional Chinese medicine, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is also used to make herbal teas and infusions that are said to promote digestion and relieve stress. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Chrysanthemum pseudanthemis as a medicinal plant.

intro: Chrysanthemum pseudanthemis, commonly known as shehan wei, is a type of flowering plant in the Asteraceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and calm the body.
